Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The standard Hire to Retire Process Typically Post Job Ad Screen Candidates Conduct Interviews Make Hiring Decision Onboard Employee Add to Payroll Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Partial Order Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means Interview and Decide can happen with no direct order between them after Screen

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In a standard process Interview must precede Decide

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The anomaly in Model 1 is the lack of Interview before Decide which allows Interview after Decide or in parallel

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The rest of the chain Decide Onboard Payroll Close is strictly sequential and correct and all mandatory steps are present

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 Edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Screen has no successor relation to Interview or Decide and is structurally disconnected from the decision path

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post Interview and Post Screen means Interview can happen before Screening or independently of Screening

Name: Validation - PE
Text: xor payroll means either Payroll or skip so Payroll becomes optional

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: loop onboarding means Onboard is executed at least once and may repeat so this is less problematic than optional Payroll

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 therefore has more severe anomalies disconnected Screening illogical ordering and optional Payroll which violate process integrity

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 is closer to the normative process because its flaw is a missing precedence constraint while Model 2 breaks logical dependencies and makes a critical activity optional
